The ABC of AVA Availability Visibility Accessibility 1. Category: A similar set of products satisfying a similar consumer need. E.g., Vermicelli, Custard, Kheer, Feerni, Sheer Khurma are all desserts. 2. Channels: Segmentation of outlets based on size, shopper mission, layout, and product range. They are named according to sales volume, scale, and customer type. 3. Primary Display: The main shelf space where the category is placed. 4. Secondary Display: Additional shelf space outside the home shelf. 5. Outlet Universe: Total number of outlets selling a relevant category in a defined area. 6. Prices: Distributor Price: Price to distributor (primary sales) Trade Price: Distributor to retailer (secondary sales) Retail Price: Retailer to consumer (tertiary sales) 7. SKUs: Unique product variants and sizes. 8. Trade Discounts/Offers: Short-term incentives to boost secondary sales. 9. Consumer Promotions: Offers to boost sales to the end consumer. 10. Coverage: Percentage of outlets being reached. 11. Outlet: A point of sale. 12. Consumer: End user of the product. 13. Shopper: Person involved in purchasing. 14. Route to Market (RTM): The method of distribution (wholesaler, retailer, etc.), distinct from "Channel." 15. Outlet Productivity / Strike Rate: Ratio of productive outlets to total visited. 16. Call Productivity: Ratio of sales calls made vs planned. 17. Range Selling: Selling multiple SKUs across a brand's portfolio. 18. Average Value per Productive Call: Total sales value divided by number of productive calls. 19. Lines per Productive Call (LPPC): Number of SKUs per order, indicating range selling. 20. Numeric Handling: % of outlets carrying the product. 21. Weighted Distribution: % of category sales from stores carrying the product. 22. Share of Shelf: Facings of a brand divided by total category facings. 23. Call to Action (CTA): POSM phrases that trigger shopper engagement (e.g., “Grab a Coke”). 24. POSM: Tools like wobblers, hangers, gondolas that draw attention at the point of sale. 25. Planogramming: Display strategy for shelf layout to boost visibility. 26. On-Shelf Availability (OSA): Compliance with planogramming guidelines. 27. Airspace Availability (ASA): Prominent display for high-visibility products. 28. 3-Second Impact: Visibility of product within three seconds of entering the store. 29. Horizontal Display: Left-to-right layout by category. 30. Vertical Display: Top-to-bottom layout by pack. 31. Potential Calculation: Estimating the market size and identifying growth opportunities. 32. Drop Size: Average quantity sold per outlet. 33. Quality of Sales (QoS): Weekly phasing of sales. Balanced QoS indicates steady sales; high QoS at month-end implies stock pushing. 34. Sales Types: Primary Sales: Company to distributor Secondary Sales: Distributor to retailer Tertiary Sales: Retailer to consumer 35. Retail Classifications: Top-End Retail: Large chains with high turnover and modern formats. Discounters/Supermarkets: Price-focused or large-scale chains. Mini Markets: Standalone stores with broad assortments. 36. Large Grocery: High-turnover local stores with wide assortments. 37. Large Bakery / GT: Local bakery chains or small general trade outlets selling baked and grocery items. 38. Wholesale: Bulk selling hubs catering to business buyers. 39. Primary Towns: Directly served areas lacking certain service infrastructures.
